Migrating All Branches from Bitbucket to GitHub
📋 Your Setup at a Glance
Source (Bitbucket)
Destination (GitHub)
Branches to Migrate
Link to read full article.
https://medium.com/@nirbhaysingh281/bitbucket-github-migration-guide-69485de438fa?sk=98b529c35d54d855ce1c98c11f604586
🛠️ Method: Manual Git Migration (Recommended for All Branches)
This approach ensures all branches + full history are migrated cleanly.
Step 1: Navigate to Your Local Repo
cd /path/to/your/proj # You're already in this directoryStep 2: Verify Current Remote (Bitbucket)
git remote -v
# You should see: origin → bitbucket.com(your Bitbucket repo)Step 3: Add GitHub as a New Remote
git remote add github https://github.com/Cgrs.git💡 We keep origin pointing to Bitbucket and add github as a new remote. This lets you push to GitHub without losing the Bitbucket connection yet.
Step 4: Fetch ALL Branches from Bitbucket
git fetch origin
